---
title: "How to Publish on Amazon KDP"
subtitle: "A Step-by-Step Guide for *Rebuilding after the Shatter*"
author: "Prepared for Gloria Ichoja Aiyebogun"
date: "April 2026"
format:
  pdf:
    documentclass: article
    papersize: a4
    mainfont: "TeX Gyre Pagella"
    sansfont: "TeX Gyre Heros"
    fontsize: 11pt
    toc: true
    toc-title: "Contents"
    number-sections: true
    geometry:
      - margin=2.5cm
    colorlinks: true
    linkcolor: "blue"
    include-in-header:
      text: |
        \usepackage{mdframed}
        \usepackage{xcolor}
        \definecolor{notebg}{RGB}{255, 248, 235}
        \definecolor{noteborder}{RGB}{155, 126, 90}
        \definecolor{stepbg}{RGB}{235, 245, 255}
        \definecolor{stepborder}{RGB}{70, 130, 180}
        \newenvironment{notebox}{
          \begin{mdframed}[backgroundcolor=notebg,linecolor=noteborder,linewidth=2pt,
            innerleftmargin=10pt,innerrightmargin=10pt,innertopmargin=8pt,innerbottommargin=8pt]
          \textbf{Important:}
        }{\end{mdframed}}
---

\newpage

# Before You Begin

This guide walks you through publishing *Rebuilding after the Shatter* on Amazon KDP as both a **Kindle ebook** and a **print paperback**. All the metadata — title, description, keywords, categories — is already written out below. You only need to copy and paste.

You will need:

- A computer with internet access
- The two files from the `_book/` folder:
  - `Rebuilding-after-the-Shatter.epub` (for the Kindle ebook)
  - `Rebuilding-after-the-Shatter.pdf` (for the paperback interior)
  - `Cover.png` (the cover image)
- A bank account or Wise/Payoneer account for royalty payments

\bigskip

**Estimated time:** 2–3 hours for both listings (ebook + paperback), including review.

\newpage

# Step 1: Create Your KDP Account

1. Open your browser and go to **https://kdp.amazon.com**

2. Click **Sign in** in the top right corner.

3. If you already have an Amazon account (for shopping), use those same login details. If not, click **Create your Amazon account** and fill in your name, email address, and a password.

4. Once signed in, KDP will ask you to complete your account setup. Click **Get started**.

\bigskip

## 1.1 Enter Your Tax Information (W-8BEN Form)

This is required for all non-US authors. It is not complicated — it simply confirms you are not a US taxpayer.

1. On the KDP dashboard, click your account name (top right) → **Account**
2. Scroll down to **Tax information** → click **View/Provide tax information**
3. Select:
   - **Individual** (not company)
   - **Non-US person**
4. You will be taken to the **W-8BEN** form. Fill in:
   - **Name:** Gloria Ichoja Aiyebogun
   - **Country of citizenship:** Nigeria
   - **Permanent address:** your address in Abuja, Nigeria
   - **Tax Identification Number:** leave blank if you do not have a Nigerian TIN, or enter it if you do
5. Check the certification box and click **Submit**

\bigskip

## 1.2 Set Up Payment (Bank Account)

1. Still in **Account**, scroll to **Payment information** → **Add bank account**

2. For Nigerian authors, **Wise** (wise.com) is the most reliable option for receiving Amazon royalties. If you do not have a Wise account:
   - Go to **wise.com** and create a free account
   - Add a USD balance — Wise will give you US bank details (routing number + account number)
   - Use those US bank details in KDP

3. Enter your bank details and click **Save**

\newpage

# Step 2: Set Up Your Author Page (Amazon Author Central)

Do this before or immediately after publishing. It takes 15 minutes and makes your book listing look professional.

1. Go to **https://authorcentral.amazon.com**
2. Sign in with the same Amazon account
3. Click **Join now**
4. Search for your name — once your book is live, it will appear here and you can claim it
5. Add:
   - **Author photo** — a clear, professional photo of Gloria
   - **Biography** — paste the text below:

\bigskip

> Gloria Ichoja Aiyebogun holds a Bachelor of Science in General and Applied Psychology and a Master's degree in Social and Terrorism Psychology, both from the University of Jos, Nigeria. She is a public health and mental health practitioner with expertise in psychological wellbeing, trauma, and community-based advocacy. For nearly a decade she worked as an online counsellor for adolescents, and has conducted research on gender-based violence with NGOs across Nigeria. She lives in Abuja, Nigeria.

\newpage

# Step 3: Publish the Kindle Ebook

On the KDP dashboard, click the yellow **Create** button → **Kindle ebook**

You will go through three panels: **Details**, **Content**, and **Pricing**.

\bigskip

## Panel 1: Ebook Details

Fill in each field exactly as shown below.

\bigskip

**Language:** English

\bigskip

**Book title:** Rebuilding after the Shatter

\bigskip

**Subtitle:** A Woman's Guide to Healing from Heartbreak, Reclaiming Her Worth, and Living in Emotional Freedom

\bigskip

**Series:** Leave blank

\bigskip

**Edition number:** Leave blank

\bigskip

**Author:** Gloria Aiyebogun

*(Click Add contributors if you want to add a second name, otherwise leave as is)*

\bigskip

**Description:** Copy and paste the text below exactly — including the HTML tags, which Amazon uses to format the listing:

\bigskip

```
Have you been lying awake at night wondering if the pain will
ever stop?

Heartbreak doesn't just break your heart — it shakes your entire
world. It leaves you questioning your worth, your future, and
everything you thought you knew about love. If you've ever felt
completely shattered by the end of a relationship,
<em>Rebuilding after the Shatter</em> was written for you.

In this compassionate and deeply practical guide, psychologist
Gloria Aiyebogun walks with you through the full arc of healing
— from the raw, aching pain of loss to the quiet, powerful
freedom of a life rebuilt on your own terms. Drawing on
psychological insight and genuine empathy, she offers not quick
fixes, but honest companionship and real tools for the journey.

<strong>Inside these pages, you will:</strong>

<ul>
<li>Understand why heartbreak hurts so deeply — and why your
    pain is completely valid</li>
<li>Learn how to break free from the silence and suppression
    that keep you stuck</li>
<li>Release the past without losing yourself in the process</li>
<li>Heal the invisible wounds of rejection, betrayal, and
    self-blame</li>
<li>Rebuild your confidence and rediscover your worth from the
    inside out</li>
<li>Find wholeness again — on your own terms, at your own
    pace</li>
<li>Open your heart to love again, wisely and without fear</li>
<li>Live in genuine emotional freedom — calm, grounded, and
    fully yourself</li>
</ul>

Each chapter ends with a personal reflection exercise that
gently guides you deeper into your own healing. This isn't just
a book to read — it's a companion to return to again and again.

<strong>You are not broken. You are becoming.</strong>

<em>Rebuilding after the Shatter</em> is for every woman who
has ever loved deeply and wondered if she would survive it.
You will — and this book will walk every step with you.
```

\bigskip

**Publishing rights:** Select **I own the copyright and I hold the necessary publishing rights**

\bigskip

**Keywords:** Enter each one on its own line:

1. healing from heartbreak
2. self-help for women
3. moving on after breakup
4. rebuilding self-worth after relationship
5. emotional healing for women
6. breakup recovery guide
7. women's empowerment and healing

\bigskip

**Categories:** Click **Set categories** and navigate to:

- **Category 1:** Browse → Self-Help → Personal Growth → Self-Esteem
- **Category 2:** Browse → Self-Help → Relationships → Love & Romance

\bigskip

**Age and grade range:** Leave both blank

\bigskip

**Pre-order:** Select **I am ready to release my book now**

\bigskip

Click **Save and continue** to move to Panel 2.

\bigskip

## Panel 2: Ebook Content

**ISBN:** Leave blank — Amazon assigns one automatically (called an ASIN)

\bigskip

**Upload manuscript:** Click **Upload ebook manuscript** and select:
$$\texttt{\_book/Rebuilding-after-the-Shatter.epub}$$

Wait for the upload to complete. Amazon will show a green tick when it is done.

\bigskip

**Kindle ebook cover:** Click **Upload your cover file** and select:
$$\texttt{Cover.png}$$

Wait for the upload. You will see a preview of the cover on the right.

\bigskip

**Kindle ebook preview:** Click **Launch previewer** to see how the book looks on different Kindle devices. Check that the chapter headings, text, and formatting all look correct. Close the previewer when done.

\bigskip

**DRM:** Select **Enable digital rights management** (protects against copying)

\bigskip

Click **Save and continue** to move to Panel 3.

\bigskip

## Panel 3: Ebook Pricing

**KDP Select enrollment:** This gives Amazon exclusivity for 90 days in exchange for promotional tools (Kindle Unlimited, Countdown Deals).

- Select **Enroll** if you want the book in Kindle Unlimited (many readers use this)
- Select **Don't enroll** if you want to sell the EPUB on other platforms (Kobo, Apple Books, etc.) at the same time

\bigskip

**Territories:** Select **All territories (worldwide rights)**

\bigskip

**Royalty and pricing:**

- Set **Primary marketplace** to your preferred currency (USD recommended for widest reach)
- Select **70% royalty** (requires a price between $2.99 and $9.99)
- Set price to **$6.99** or **$7.99** — competitive for self-help ebooks of this length
- Amazon will automatically calculate prices for other marketplaces

\bigskip

**Matchbook price:** Leave blank

\bigskip

**Kindle book lending:** Leave enabled (standard)

\bigskip

Click **Publish your Kindle ebook**.

Amazon will review the book within **24–72 hours** and send you an email when it is live.

\newpage

# Step 4: Publish the Paperback

On the KDP dashboard, click **Create** → **Paperback**

\bigskip

## Panel 1: Paperback Details

Fill in the same **title, subtitle, author, description, keywords, and categories** as the ebook (see Step 3 above — all fields are identical).

\bigskip

Click **Save and continue**.

\bigskip

## Panel 2: Paperback Content

**ISBN:** Click **Get a free KDP ISBN**. Amazon will assign one. Note: this means the publisher name shown on Amazon will be *Independently published*. This is fine and very common.

\bigskip

**Publication date:** Leave blank (KDP fills this automatically)

\bigskip

**Print options:**

| Setting | Choose |
|---|---|
| Interior type | Black & white |
| Paper colour | White |
| Trim size | 6 x 9 in (15.24 x 22.86 cm) |
| Bleed settings | No bleed |
| Paperback cover finish | Matte |

\bigskip

**Upload manuscript:** Click **Upload paperback manuscript** and select:
$$\texttt{\_book/Rebuilding-after-the-Shatter.pdf}$$

Wait for Amazon to process the file. It will check the page count and margins automatically. You should see a green tick with the page count confirmed.

\bigskip

**Book cover:** Click **Launch Cover Creator**

Inside Cover Creator:

1. Click **Use your own image** and upload `Cover.png`
2. KDP will place it as the front cover automatically
3. Select the **back cover** area and paste the following blurb:

\bigskip

> Heartbreak doesn't just break your heart. It shakes your identity, silences your voice, and leaves you wondering if you will ever feel whole again.
>
> *Rebuilding after the Shatter* is a compassionate, practical guide for every woman who has loved deeply and been left broken. Written by a psychologist with nearly a decade of counselling experience, this book walks with you through the full journey of healing — from understanding your pain, to releasing the past, to reclaiming your worth and opening your heart again.
>
> With eight gentle chapters and a personal reflection exercise in each one, this is not a book you simply read. It is a companion you return to — on the hard days, the quiet days, and the days when you finally begin to feel like yourself again.
>
> **You are not broken. You are becoming.**

\bigskip

4. Leave the **bottom-right corner of the back cover blank** — Amazon places the barcode there automatically
5. Set the spine colour to match the cover background (dark purple)
6. Click **Save and submit**

\bigskip

**Preview:** Click **Launch previewer** and flip through the interior pages. Check the first and last pages look correct.

\bigskip

Click **Save and continue**.

\bigskip

## Panel 3: Paperback Pricing

**Territories:** Select **All territories (worldwide rights)**

\bigskip

**Primary marketplace:** USD

\bigskip

**Pricing:**

- KDP will show you the **minimum list price** based on printing cost (approximately $5.50 for 48 pages at 6×9)
- Set your price to **$9.99** — this earns a reasonable royalty while staying accessible
- You can use the **royalty calculator** on the same page to see your exact earnings per copy

\bigskip

Click **Publish your paperback**.

Amazon reviews paperbacks within **72 hours** and emails you when it is live.

\newpage

# Step 5: Link Both Listings on Author Central

Once both the ebook and paperback are live (you will receive two confirmation emails):

1. Go back to **https://authorcentral.amazon.com**
2. Click **Books** → **Add more books**
3. Search for *Rebuilding after the Shatter* — both listings should appear
4. Click **This is my book** on each one
5. Both will now appear on Gloria's author page

Customers visiting the Amazon listing will see both the ebook and paperback options, and Gloria's author profile will show both.

\newpage

# Final Checklist

Work through this list before clicking Publish on each format.

\bigskip

**Account setup**

- [ ] KDP account created at kdp.amazon.com
- [ ] W-8BEN tax form submitted
- [ ] Bank / Wise account linked for royalty payments
- [ ] Amazon Author Central page created with photo and bio

\bigskip

**Kindle ebook**

- [ ] Title and subtitle entered correctly
- [ ] Description pasted with HTML formatting
- [ ] All 7 keywords entered
- [ ] Both categories selected
- [ ] EPUB uploaded: `Rebuilding-after-the-Shatter.epub`
- [ ] Cover uploaded: `Cover.png`
- [ ] Previewer checked — formatting looks correct
- [ ] Price set to $6.99 or $7.99
- [ ] Submitted

\bigskip

**Paperback**

- [ ] Free KDP ISBN obtained
- [ ] Print options set (6×9, B&W, white paper, matte cover)
- [ ] PDF uploaded: `Rebuilding-after-the-Shatter.pdf`
- [ ] Back cover blurb added in Cover Creator
- [ ] Previewer checked — interior looks correct
- [ ] Price set to $9.99
- [ ] Submitted

\bigskip

**After publishing**

- [ ] Both confirmation emails received from Amazon
- [ ] Both listings claimed on Author Central
- [ ] Listings visible on amazon.com
- [ ] Amazon links added to the README on GitHub
